annotations.label: Poincaré recurrence theorem

anchor.snippet: Poincaré recurrence theorem

annotations.note: Stated as a Poincaré recurrence theorem for conservative maps, with a companion result `ae_mem_imp_frequently_image_mem` giving the infinite-return form.

annotations.label: Ergodic transformation

anchor.snippet: Then T is ergodic if for every E in

annotations.note: `Ergodic f μ` extends `MeasurePreserving` and `PreErgodic`, requiring measurable invariant sets to be null or conull.

annotations.label: Essentially invariant set

anchor.snippet: The condition that the symmetric difference be measure zero is called being essentially invariant

annotations.note: Mathlib does not name the notion of an essentially invariant set, but `QuasiErgodic.ae_empty_or_univ₀` uses the almost-everywhere invariance condition `f ⁻¹' s =ᵐ[μ] s` directly.

annotations.label: Irrational rotation of the circle is ergodic

anchor.snippet: An irrational rotation of the circle

annotations.note: Characterizes ergodicity of rotation by `a` on `AddCircle p` by `addOrderOf a = 0`, i.e. `a/p` is irrational.

annotations.label: Ergodicity of group automorphisms of compact abelian groups

anchor.snippet: Let G be a compact abelian group

annotations.note: Mathlib gives a general criterion for a continuous surjective endomorphism of a compact group to be ergodic via density of preimages of 1, but no direct characterization for compact abelian groups via the dual.

annotations.label: Bernoulli shift is ergodic

anchor.snippet: A Bernoulli shift is ergodic

annotations.note: Grep found no Bernoulli shift or shift-ergodicity statement in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Non-ergodicity of systems with non-constant first integral

anchor.snippet: A system with a compact phase space which has a non-constant first integral cannot be ergodic

annotations.note: No notion of `firstIntegral` or related non-ergodicity criterion exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pointwise (Birkhoff) ergodic theorem

anchor.snippet: the pointwise or strong ergodic theorem states that the limit in the definition of the time average

annotations.note: Birkhoff averages are defined, but no pointwise a.e. convergence theorem (Birkhoff's theorem) is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kingman's subadditive ergodic theorem

anchor.snippet: A generalization of Birkhoff's theorem is Kingman's subadditive ergodic theorem

annotations.note: No Kingman subadditive ergodic theorem is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Birkhoff–Khinchin theorem

anchor.snippet: Let ƒ be measurable

annotations.note: This is the L¹ pointwise ergodic theorem; not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pointwise Ergodic Theorem (corollary)

anchor.snippet: Corollary

annotations.note: Equality of time and space averages for ergodic systems is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Von Neumann's mean ergodic theorem

anchor.snippet: Let U be a unitary operator on a Hilbert space

annotations.note: Proven for any contracting continuous linear operator on a Hilbert space, with Birkhoff averages converging to the orthogonal projection onto the fixed-point subspace.

annotations.label: Mean ergodic theorem for one-parameter unitary groups

anchor.snippet: In another form of the mean ergodic theorem, let

annotations.note: Mathlib has only the discrete-time mean ergodic theorem, not the continuous one-parameter version.

annotations.label: Convergence of ergodic means in Lp

anchor.section: Convergence of the ergodic means in the L p norms
anchor.snippet: converge to the projector

annotations.note: No Lp-norm convergence statement for ergodic means exists in Mathlib outside the Hilbert space case.

annotations.label: Wiener–Yoshida–Kakutani ergodic dominated convergence theorem

anchor.section: Convergence of the ergodic means in the L p norms
anchor.snippet: Wiener–Yoshida–Kakutani ergodic dominated convergence theorem

annotations.note: No Wiener–Yoshida–Kakutani style maximal/dominated theorem for ergodic averages found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sojourn time

anchor.snippet: The time spent in a measurable set A is called the sojourn time

annotations.note: No notion of sojourn time appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Relative measure equals mean sojourn time

anchor.snippet: the relative measure of A is equal to the mean sojourn time

annotations.note: This is a corollary of the pointwise ergodic theorem, which is not formalized.

annotations.label: Occurrence times and recurrence times

anchor.snippet: The occurrence times of a measurable set

annotations.note: No notion of occurrence/recurrence times for measurable sets is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Average recurrence time inversely proportional to measure

anchor.snippet: the average recurrence time of A is inversely proportional to the measure of A

annotations.note: Kac's lemma is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hopf's ergodicity of geodesic flow on negatively curved manifolds

anchor.snippet: The ergodicity of the geodesic flow on compact Riemann surfaces of variable negative curvature

annotations.note: No geodesic-flow ergodicity result for negatively curved manifolds exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mautner's ergodicity on symmetric spaces

anchor.snippet: Ergodicity of the geodesic flow on Riemannian symmetric spaces was demonstrated by F. I. Mautner

annotations.note: Mautner's theorem on ergodicity over symmetric spaces is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Anosov–Sinai ergodicity theorem

anchor.snippet: Anosov and Ya. G. Sinai proved ergodicity of the geodesic flow

annotations.note: No Anosov–Sinai geodesic-flow ergodicity result is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Moore's ergodicity criterion for homogeneous flows

anchor.snippet: A simple criterion for the ergodicity of a homogeneous flow on a homogeneous space of a semisimple Lie group

annotations.note: Moore's ergodicity criterion for semisimple Lie group actions is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hedlund's horocycle flow theorem

anchor.snippet: Hedlund proved that the horocycle flow on a compact hyperbolic surface is minimal and ergodic

annotations.note: No horocycle flow definition or Hedlund result exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Furstenberg's unique ergodicity of horocycle flow

anchor.snippet: Unique ergodicity of the flow was established by Hillel Furstenberg

annotations.note: Unique ergodicity is not defined in Mathlib, nor is this Furstenberg result.

annotations.label: Ratner's theorems

anchor.snippet: Ratner's theorems provide a major generalization of ergodicity for unipotent flows

annotations.note: Ratner's measure-classification and orbit-closure theorems are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lindenstrauss measure rigidity result

anchor.snippet: An important partial result

annotations.note: Lindenstrauss's measure rigidity theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.
